resources.name: Scotch Whisky Association
resources.notes: Scotch legal definitions, distillery map, policy updates, export context.

resources.name: Drinks Ireland - Irish Whiskey Association
resources.notes: Irish whiskey category guidance, policy context, and links to practical learning resources.

resources.name: Irish Whiskey 360
resources.notes: Distillery discovery map and cultural framing for Irish whiskey storytelling.

resources.name: American Single Malt Whiskey Commission
resources.notes: Useful for category identity, standards advocacy, and style positioning in the US market.

resources.name: American Craft Spirits Association
resources.notes: Advocacy and practical support for small craft producers; useful for one-person and micro teams.

resources.name: TTB Distilled Spirits
resources.notes: US federal labeling, formula, and permitting requirements for distilled spirits.

resources.name: UK HMRC - Excise Notice 39
resources.notes: Core UK rules for spirits production and records; essential operations reference for small sites.

resources.name: European Commission - GIView
resources.notes: Official EU geographical indication register useful for category and labeling boundaries.

resources.name: Institute of Brewing and Distilling
resources.notes: Structured technical education for distillation science and process control.

resources.name: The Distiller (Moonshine University)
resources.notes: Training-oriented materials and practical process guidance for startup and small distillers.

resources.name: Whisky Science
resources.notes: Detailed technical articles on chemistry and process variables affecting spirit character.

resources.name: Whisky Magazine
resources.notes: Interviews, production reporting, and market context across regions.

resources.name: Whisky Advocate
resources.notes: Long-form editorial and category explainers with useful context on evolving consumer expectations.

resources.name: The Whisky Exchange - Inspiration
resources.notes: Accessible explainers that help frame products for non-expert customers.

resources.name: ScotchWhisky.com
resources.notes: Distillery profiles and historical summaries useful for comparative study.

resources.name: Malt Review
resources.notes: Independent criticism and transparent review style useful for understanding informed enthusiast expectations.

resources.name: Whisky Notes
resources.notes: Detailed tasting and historical references that help compare style claims to sensory outcomes.

resources.name: Whiskey Raiders
resources.notes: Market pulse and product awareness; use as supplemental, not primary technical source.

resources.name: Bourbon Pursuit
resources.notes: Interviews and stories that help understand bourbon community and communication tone.

resources.name: Distiller Magazine
resources.notes: Focused practical operations content from startup to scale, including maintenance and safety themes.

resources.name: StillDragon Learn
resources.notes: Equipment-focused practical notes and distilling process discussions.

resources.name: CaskNet
resources.notes: Cask sourcing and management context useful for planning maturation strategy.

resources.name: World Cooperage Resources
resources.notes: Oak, toasting, and barrel information for maturation planning.

resources.name: Whisky Cast
resources.notes: Industry interviews and reports that help with market language and brand communication awareness.

resources.name: The Glenlivet - Whisky School
resources.notes: Brand-led educational content; useful for understanding consumer-friendly process explanations.

resources.name: National Geographic - History of Whiskey
resources.notes: General public history context; use for broad framing, then verify with specialist sources.

resources.name: Whisky.com Knowledge
resources.notes: Structured production explainers suitable for rapid refresh before practical planning.

resources.name: Spirits & Cocktails Australia
resources.notes: Peak body for the Australian spirits industry; policy, advocacy, and category guidance for all Australian producers including emerging craft operations.

resources.name: Australian Distillers Association
resources.notes: Specialist body for independent and craft Australian distillers; supports single-person operations with compliance guidance, community, and practical resources for members.

resources.name: Australian Taxation Office - Excise for Alcohol
resources.notes: Definitive source for Australian excise rates, duty calculation, producer rebate eligibility, and reporting obligations; the producer rebate scheme is a critical tool for small Australian distilleries.

resources.name: Australian Border Force - Spirits Excise
resources.notes: Manufacturer licence applications, excise bonds, record-keeping requirements, and audit obligations for spirits production in Australia.

resources.name: Food Standards Australia New Zealand (FSANZ)
resources.notes: Labelling requirements for spirits sold in Australia and New Zealand; essential for bottle design, composition claims, allergen declarations, and origin statements.

resources.name: IP Australia - Geographical Indications for Spirits
resources.notes: Australia's GI framework for spirits; particularly important for Tasmanian producers making provenance claims and for any distillery seeking protected regional identity.

resources.name: Australian Wine Research Institute (AWRI)
resources.notes: World-class research on fermentation chemistry, yeast science, and flavour development directly applicable to spirit base wash quality and character; Australian climate effects on maturation are also documented.

resources.name: Barley Australia
resources.notes: Varietal data, quality specifications, and agronomic research on Australian malting barley; useful for ingredient sourcing decisions and provenance storytelling around local grain.

resources.name: Whisky Waffle
resources.notes: Australian-founded whisky blog and podcast with in-depth coverage of local producers; an excellent reference for how Australian whisky culture is communicated to enthusiast audiences.

resources.name: The Whisky List
resources.notes: Australia's leading independent whisky review and discovery platform; deep and consistent coverage of local craft producers makes it the first reference for Australian market context.

resources.name: Nip of Courage
resources.notes: Independent Australian whisky advocacy and review with a strong focus on small and emerging Australian producers; useful for understanding enthusiast expectations of local craft releases.

resources.name: The Whisky Club Australia
resources.notes: Australia's largest whisky subscription community; useful for understanding how Australian consumers engage with local releases, what editorial framing resonates, and which producer stories land well.

resources.name: Drinks Trade
resources.notes: Australian drinks trade media covering spirits industry news, distillery launches, regulatory changes, distributor moves, and on-premise market trends.

resources.name: Japan Spirits & Liqueurs Makers Association (JSLA)
resources.notes: The primary Japanese spirits industry body; issued the landmark 2021 whisky labelling standards (effective April 2024) that define what can be called Japanese whisky. Essential context for understanding the category's identity and regulatory framework. Site is primarily Japanese-language.

resources.name: Japan National Tax Agency - Whisky Labelling Standards
resources.notes: The NTA oversees alcohol licensing, excise tax, and the 2021 whisky standards requiring Japanese whisky to be mashed, fermented, distilled, matured, and bottled in Japan using Japanese water. Critical reference for understanding what the 'Japanese whisky' category legally means from April 2024.

resources.name: Japan Customs - Alcoholic Beverages
resources.notes: Import, export, and tariff documentation for spirits entering and leaving Japan; relevant for understanding distribution channels, import duties on foreign casks and raw materials, and export compliance for Japanese whisky.

resources.name: National Research Institute of Brewing (NRIB)
resources.notes: Japan's public brewing and distilling research institution; publishes technical research on fermentation, yeast strains, flavour chemistry, and spirits quality analysis. Primary source for Japanese scientific literature on whisky production. Site is primarily Japanese-language but key papers are available in English.

resources.name: Mizunara Oak Research & Cask Science
resources.notes: Aggregated academic papers on Quercus mongolica (mizunara), including lactone profiles, porosity challenges, incense and sandalwood flavour compounds, and why it requires extended maturation. Foundational for understanding Japanese whisky's most distinctive maturation character.

resources.name: Nonjatta - Japanese Whisky Blog
resources.notes: The definitive English-language source on Japanese whisky; Stefan Van Eycken's long-running blog covers the full history of Suntory, Nikka, and the modern craft revival, distillery visits, regulations, and bottling records. Essential primary reference for any serious study of Japanese whisky.

resources.name: WhiskyFun - Japan Archive
resources.notes: Serge Valentin's two-decade archive of detailed Japanese whisky tasting notes across all major distilleries and independent bottlings; an invaluable longitudinal record of how Japanese whisky character has evolved and how specific distilleries express themselves.

resources.name: The Spirits Business - Japanese Whisky
resources.notes: Trade journalism covering Japanese whisky launches, investment activity, distillery expansions, global distribution deals, and supply shortage coverage; useful for market context and understanding how Japanese whisky is communicated to the global trade.

resources.name: Whisky Auctioneer - Japanese Whisky Intelligence
resources.notes: Secondary market pricing and collector demand signals for Japanese whisky; useful for understanding which distilleries and vintages command premium and how scarcity narratives around rare Japanese releases shape consumer perception.

resources.name: Chichibu Distillery - Ichiro's Malt
resources.notes: Chichibu, founded by Ichiro Akuto in 2008, is the benchmark modern Japanese craft distillery built by a single founder. Akuto's documented approach to floor malting, small pot still design, mizunara maturation, and direct-to-consumer community building is the most referenced example of a Japanese micro-distillery done right.
